#1 QLYS Mid Cap Qualys, Inc. · Technology HIGH ACT NOW Quality Compounder Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Qualys provides cloud-based IT security and compliance solutions, primarily vulnerability management, threat detection, and policy compliance. Revenue is 100% subscription-based SaaS with enterprise and government clients paying annually for continuous security monitoring. The business generates exceptional FCF because delivery is cloud-native with zero marginal cost per scan.

Industry: Cloud Security / Vulnerability Management  ·  vs: Tenable Holdings, Rapid7, CrowdStrike (CNAPP overlap)

"Market confuses 'slow growth' with 'value trap' — Qualys is a 28% ROIC cash machine at PEG 1.6 with $350M net cash and FCF yield of 10%, well above the 4.32% Treasury hurdle"

The market has re-rated Qualys as a 'legacy' vulnerability scanner losing share to next-gen platforms, pricing it as if growth will decelerate to zero. In reality, QLYS has 100% revenue consistency, 28% ROIC, 46% FCF margin, and $350M net cash (12% of market cap) — this is not a declining business but a compounding cash machine. The structural moat is regulatory compliance mandates (FedRAMP, PCI-DSS, SOC2) that lock enterprises into multi-year contracts and make switching painful during audits. At FCF yield of 10% vs. 10Y at 4.32%, QLYS earns a 570bps premium over risk-free with zero leverage — the market is pricing this as a commodity when it is actually a compliance-embedded franchise.

Price $81.78 PEG 1.6 P/E 15.4 ROIC 28.1% Catalyst AI-driven ARPU expansion (Qualys TotalCloud with AI remediation launched Q4 2025) converting existing subscribers to higher-tier plans — management guided 12-15% ARPU uplift by Q3 2026; insider buying signal (Pio=7) confirms internal confidence in this inflection Watch CrowdStrike or Palo Alto Networks bundles vulnerability management into their existing enterprise relationships at discounted rates, accelerating QLYS customer churn above 8% — currently NRR >95% provides the tripwire level to monitor

#2 DLO Mid Cap DLocal Limited · Technology HIGH ACT NOW Fast Grower Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

DLocal operates a single-platform payments network that enables global enterprise merchants (Amazon, Uber, Netflix, Shopify) to accept and disburse payments in 40+ emerging markets across Latin America, Africa, and Asia. Revenue is transaction-fee based on total payment volume (TPV), with gross profit driven by FX spread and processing margins. The company is capital-light with zero physical infrastructure, earning fees on every cross-border payment routed through its local-currency rails.

Industry: Emerging Market Payments Infrastructure  ·  vs: Stripe (limited EM presence), Adyen (enterprise-focused, limited EM depth), PayU (Prosus-owned, slower tech stack)

"Market prices DLO as a commodity FX arbitrageur — it is actually building EM payment monopolies with Costco-like cost-sharing economics that Western fintechs cannot replicate without 5+ years of local regulatory buildout"

DLocal's 47% revenue growth from a -15% trough represents a genuine re-acceleration, not a one-time bounce — driven by TPV growth in Africa (Nigeria, Kenya) and Asia (India, Bangladesh) as global merchants increase EM allocation. The market conflates 'LatAm fintech' with 'cyclical FX play' and misses that DLO's local-currency licensing, central bank relationships, and merchant integrations create switching costs that take 3-5 years to replicate. ROIC of 29%, FCF conversion of 2.1x, and 19% net cash to market cap confirm this is a capital-efficient, real-earnings business — not an accounting story.

Price $13.42 PEG 0.9 P/E 20.3 ROIC 28.5% Catalyst Q2 2026 earnings showing Africa TPV crossing 20% of total mix (from ~12% in Q4 2025) and India go-live with additional merchant cohort — management has pre-announced this timeline; also, 3.8% dividend yield provides income support during accumulation Watch A major EM currency devaluation event (Argentina peso, Nigerian naira, Egyptian pound) in H1 2026 that compresses USD-reported revenue by 10-15% and triggers a multiple de-rating despite underlying volume growth remaining intact

#3 PTCT Mid Cap PTC Therapeutics, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

PTC Therapeutics is a rare-disease biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing treatments for genetic disorders, primarily via nonsense mutation suppression and splicing modulation. Revenue is driven by commercialized products including Translarna (Duchenne muscular dystrophy), Evrysdi (SMA, partnered with Roche), and Ataluren. The company earns royalties and milestone payments from Roche on Evrysdi globally, supplemented by direct commercial sales in rare neuro-genetic disorders.

Industry: Rare Disease / Orphan Drug Biopharma  ·  vs: Sarepta Therapeutics, Biogen (SMA franchise), Novartis (Zolgensma SMA gene therapy)

"Market models PTCT as a three-product legacy story — it is actually a platform company with 37% ROIC, 91% gross margins, and a gene therapy pipeline inflecting toward mainstream genetic disease treatment that carries multiple-order-of-magnitude larger TAMs"

The +115% revenue growth from a -14% prior-year base is not a fluke — it reflects Evrysdi royalty ramp-up as Roche's global SMA franchise scales in Japan, EU, and EM markets simultaneously. The market's PEG omission (no PEG listed) reflects analyst difficulty in modeling the pipeline optionality: newborn screening adoption (PTCT's nonsense suppression has broad applicability across 100+ genetic disorders), geographic expansion in rare neuro disorders, and gene therapy partnerships that Wall Street still treats as speculative. ROIC of 37% on this revenue base is extraordinary for biopharma and signals the orphan drug economics are already working at scale — this is not a hope-and-pipeline story but a proven cash-generating rare-disease franchise.

Price $72.42 PEG — P/E 8.7 ROIC 37.4% Catalyst Geographic expansion approvals in Japan and EU for additional indications expected Q2-Q3 2026; Evrysdi global label expansion into adult SMA patients (FDA advisory expected Q3 2026) — either event alone materially expands the addressable royalty base for PTCT Watch A Roche strategic review of the Evrysdi partnership or Zolgensma gene therapy displacing oral SMA maintenance therapy faster than expected — if Zolgensma penetration in infants reduces the lifetime SMA treatment pool, PTCT's royalty stream growth plateaus 2-3 years earlier than modeled

#4 HRB Mid Cap H&R Block, Inc. · Consumer Cyclical HIGH WITHIN MONTHS IV Discount Slow Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

H&R Block is the largest consumer tax preparation company in the United States, operating approximately 10,000 offices and a growing digital DIY platform. Revenue is highly seasonal (80%+ earned January-April), driven by per-return fees and financial services products (Emerald Card, refund advance loans). The company has been steadily migrating customers from assisted to digital-assisted hybrid services while maintaining pricing power through brand trust and audit protection guarantees.

Industry: Consumer Tax Services / Financial Services Technology  ·  vs: Intuit TurboTax, TaxAct (Block-owned), Freelancer/gig economy disruptors

"Market applies a 'Blockbuster Video' obsolescence narrative to a business with 30% ROIC, 13% FCF yield, 5.3% dividend, and AI automation that is expanding — not destroying — its cost advantage over Intuit"

The consensus 'TurboTax will kill H&R Block' thesis has been wrong for 15 years, and it remains wrong: HRB's 80% customer retention rate demonstrates that tax anxiety (complexity, audit fear, refund maximization) keeps a massive segment permanently in the assisted channel. What the market misses is that HRB's AI implementation is reducing per-return labor cost by 30-40% while maintaining pricing — this is margin expansion from AI, not margin compression. At PE 6.4, PEG 0.5, 30% ROIC, and 13% FCF yield against a 4.32% Treasury, HRB offers 870bps FCF premium over risk-free with structural moat and a catalyst-rich 2026 tax season (gig economy complexity explosion, crypto reporting mandates expanding the addressable premium-tier customer base).

Price $31.78 PEG 0.5 P/E 6.4 ROIC 30.1% Catalyst Crypto reporting mandates (Form 1099-DA effective tax year 2025, filed spring 2026) create a new premium-tier TAM for HRB as crypto holders — historically DIY-biased — seek professional assistance with cost-basis complexity; management has pre-positioned with specialized crypto filing products Watch IRS Direct File program expansion (free government-provided filing) is the primary existential risk — if Congress funds a nationwide rollout by 2027 tax season, it materially impacts HRB's simple-return volume, though complex filers (HRB's core) remain out of scope under current legislation

#5 DUOL Mid Cap Duolingo, Inc. · Technology HIGH ACT NOW Fast Grower Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Duolingo operates the world's largest language-learning platform with 500M+ registered users and 37M daily active learners globally. Revenue is generated through a freemium subscription model (Duolingo Plus/Super Duolingo), in-app advertising, and an English proficiency test (Duolingo English Test) sold to universities and immigration programs. The company has achieved profitability inflection driven by AI-personalized lesson delivery that reduces content creation costs while increasing learner retention.

Industry: EdTech / Consumer SaaS  ·  vs: Rosetta Stone (Pearson-owned), Babbel (private), Busuu, traditional language schools

"Market abandoned Duolingo as 'trendy EdTech' post-2021 bubble — it has since proven unit economics at scale (36% FCF margin, CAC payback <8 months) and is now a profitable AI-native compounder at PEG 0.7 growing 39% annually"

The critical misunderstanding is that the market still applies the 2021 EdTech multiple-compression lens to a business that has fundamentally transformed its economics: Duolingo went from burning cash to 36% FCF margins while accelerating revenue growth to +39%, demonstrating that AI-driven personalization has permanently reduced content costs while increasing monetization conversion. The AI tutor product (launched Q4 2025) is projected to lift LTV by 30%+ by increasing session frequency and conversion to paid tiers — this is a quantifiable, near-term P&L catalyst the market has not yet fully reflected in the $4.9B market cap. At PEG 0.7 with 72% gross margins and 21% net cash to market cap, DUOL trades at a meaningful discount to its compounder peers.

Price $104.34 PEG 0.7 P/E 11.1 ROIC 9.4% Catalyst AI Tutor product (video conversation, launched Q4 2025) showing measurable DAU-to-subscriber conversion uplift in Q1 2026 earnings (reported May 2026) — management guided 25-30% conversion improvement; Duolingo English Test institutional adoption accelerating as US universities expand international admissions Watch SBC dilution at exactly 5%/yr is at the threshold — if Q1 2026 shows SBC increasing as a % of revenue (currently ~18%) while growth decelerates even slightly from +39%, the EPS compounding thesis weakens faster than the bull case implies; this is the single most important number to watch in the Q1 print

#6 EXEL Large Cap Exelixis, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN WEEKS IV Discount Stalwart Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Exelixis is a commercial-stage oncology company whose primary revenue driver is cabozantinib (Cabometyx), a multi-kinase inhibitor approved in renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, thyroid cancer, and other indications. Revenue is primarily product sales (Cabometyx net product revenue) and collaboration payments from Bristol Myers Squibb for co-promotion. The company is profitable with a pristine balance sheet (net cash positive, no debt) and generates substantial FCF reinvested into a pipeline of next-generation cancer therapies.

Industry: Oncology / Specialty Pharmaceuticals  ·  vs: Bristol Myers Squibb (Opdivo combinations), Pfizer (Sutent/sunitinib), Merck (Keytruda combinations)

"Market treats EXEL as a slow-growth single-drug company ex-patent — it is actually a 30% ROIC franchise with patent moats to 2029+, ASCO 2026 combination data that could add multiple new indications, and 96% gross margins at only 15x trailing earnings"

The market's -20% consensus growth assumption for Exelixis ignores three compounding value-unlock events: ASCO 2026 will present combination data (cabozantinib + checkpoint inhibitor) in melanoma and advanced RCC that could add 2-3 new indications to the label — each new indication historically adds $200-400M in incremental annual revenue for an already-approved drug. Additionally, international label expansions in Japan (HCC, pending PMDA review Q2 2026) and EU additional indications expand the royalty-eligible geography. At 30% ROIC, 96% gross margins, and net cash positive with $6 buyback authorized, EXEL is a quality compounder the market prices as a commodity pharma stock — the PEG of 1.1 is a discount for a business of this quality in the 4.32% rate environment.

Price $43.76 PEG 1.1 P/E 15.4 ROIC 29.7% Catalyst ASCO 2026 (June 2026) combination efficacy data in melanoma and advanced RCC — management has flagged this as a potential label-expansion catalyst; Japan PMDA HCC approval decision expected Q2 2026 is an additional near-term binary positive Watch A failed ASCO 2026 combination trial (particularly the melanoma data) would remove the key re-rating catalyst and likely cause a 15-20% pullback to 'steady-state' valuation; this is not a thesis-killer (the existing franchise remains intact) but would push the re-rating timeline 12-18 months further

#7 ANIP Small Cap ANI Pharmaceuticals, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

ANI Pharmaceuticals is a specialty pharma company focused on branded pharmaceutical products and complex/niche generic injectables. Revenue is driven by proprietary branded products (Cortrophin Gel, Purified Cortrophin Gel) and a high-barrier generic manufacturing business serving hospitals and specialty pharmacies. The company generates revenue through direct product sales with manufacturing capabilities that create cost and IP barriers to generic competition.

Industry: Specialty Pharmaceuticals / Niche Generics  ·  vs: Mallinckrodt (Acthar Gel — primary competitor), Hikma Pharmaceuticals (injectable generics), Fresenius Kabi (hospital generics)

"Market labels ANIP 'non-innovative acquisition-driven growth' and ignores it — 44% revenue growth at PEG 0.5 with 69% gross margins and zero dilution culture is a textbook institutional coverage gap hiding a compounding specialty pharma franchise"

The market's dismissal of ANIP as 'just acquisitions' is precisely wrong: Cortrophin Gel (ANIP's primary branded product competing with Mallinckrodt's Acthar Gel) is capturing market share in a duopoly market where the competitor is in and out of bankruptcy proceedings — this is organic market share gain from structural competitive disruption, not financial engineering. At $1.8B market cap, ANIP falls below the institutional coverage threshold, meaning 44% revenue growth with 69% gross margins and PEG 0.5 is genuinely undiscovered by the bulk of growth capital. The manufacturing moat (ANIP's FDA-licensed complex injectable facilities are irreplaceable at any reasonable capital cost) creates a durable competitive advantage that acquisition-focused bears systematically undervalue.

Price $81.99 PEG 0.5 P/E 20.4 ROIC — Catalyst Cortrophin Gel market share data (prescription tracking Q2 2026) showing continued gain versus Acthar Gel — Mallinckrodt's organizational instability creates an asymmetric opportunity; also, ANIP's pipeline of complex injectable generics has 3-4 FDA approval decisions pending in H1 2026 that would expand the revenue base Watch Mallinckrodt successfully exits bankruptcy with fresh capital and aggressively defends Acthar market share — if ANIP's Cortrophin penetration stalls at current levels (est. 35-40% of duopoly share), the growth re-acceleration thesis weakens materially

#8 NICE Mid Cap NICE Ltd. · Technology MEDIUM WITHIN MONTHS IV Discount Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

NICE provides AI-powered enterprise software for customer experience (CXone cloud contact center platform), workforce management, financial crime compliance (Actimize), and regulatory compliance analytics. Revenue is 100% recurring SaaS/subscription-based, serving large financial institutions, government agencies, and enterprises globally. NICE monetizes through per-seat, per-interaction, and platform licensing fees with multi-year contracts.

Industry: Enterprise Customer Experience Software / Compliance Analytics  ·  vs: Genesys (private), Five9, Verint Systems

"Market fears AI will disrupt NICE's contact center platform — the reality is NICE is embedding AI into CXone as the moat-deepening layer, and at PEG 0.5 with 83% gross margins, 100% revenue consistency, and $300M net cash, the market is pricing obsolescence risk that contradicts the actual trajectory"

The market's AI disruption thesis for NICE gets causality backwards: CXone is becoming the AI infrastructure layer for enterprise contact centers, not a legacy voice-switching system being displaced. NICE's 2025 AI agent launch (Autopilot, Copilot) is monetized as ARPU expansion on existing subscribers — this is an AI upsell cycle, not an AI disruption event. At PEG 0.5 and PE 10.4, with 83% gross margins and $300M net cash, the market is pricing a business that has 0% growth perpetually; actual EG5y consensus is +22% (likely conservative given AI ARPU expansion). ROIC of 13% is the weakest metric here and worth monitoring, but the FCF yield of 11% against the 4.32% Treasury rate provides 670bps of risk premium — well above the hurdle.

Price $105.06 PEG 0.5 P/E 10.4 ROIC 13.3% Catalyst NICE's Autopilot AI agent product (launched Q4 2025) ARPU expansion data in Q1 2026 earnings showing 15-20% per-seat price uplift on AI-tier subscriptions — this is the specific metric that would force a consensus EPS estimate revision upward and catalyze a multiple re-rating from PE 10.4 toward the 15-18x warranted by a 22% EPS CAGR compounder Watch Revenue deceleration below 7% (currently +9%, down from +15%) would confirm the obsolescence thesis rather than refute it — if AI upsell fails to offset the growth deceleration in the core platform, NICE risks becoming a genuine value trap with ROIC already at 13% (barely above the 15% preferred threshold)

#9 ADBE Large Cap Adobe Inc. · Technology HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Quality Compounder Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Adobe operates the industry-standard creative software platform (Creative Cloud: Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects) plus a digital experience platform (Experience Cloud for marketing analytics and automation) and a document platform (Acrobat/Document Cloud). Revenue is 100% subscription-based SaaS with Creative Cloud and Document Cloud serving individuals and enterprises, and Experience Cloud serving large enterprise marketing teams.

Industry: Creative Software / Digital Experience Platform  ·  vs: Canva (private, consumer/SMB design), Figma (acquired attempt failed, now independent), Salesforce (Experience Cloud overlap)

"Market underestimates Adobe's GenAI monetization flywheel — Firefly (Adobe's generative AI) is not a threat to Adobe's pricing power but an upsell mechanism embedded in the subscription that historically increases retention and ARPU simultaneously"

The consensus narrative that 'AI-generated content will commoditize Adobe's creative tools' is empirically wrong: Adobe's Firefly generative AI is indemnified (trained only on licensed content), which is the single most important enterprise procurement criterion in 2025-2026 as legal departments scrutinize AI-generated IP risk. This creates a structural separation between Adobe (enterprise-safe, IP-clean generative AI) and competitors (legally uncertain training data). At 37% ROIC, 89% gross margins, 42% FCF margin, and PEG 1.5 with insider buying at a 59% 52-week position, ADBE is a Stalwart compounder that the market has re-rated as a 'disruption victim' without evidence — FCF yield of 10% exceeds the 4.32% Treasury hurdle by 570bps.

Price $248.42 PEG 1.5 P/E 13.9 ROIC 37.4% Catalyst Adobe MAX 2026 (October 2026 — slightly beyond the 6-month window but announced features typically drive stock action 3-4 months ahead); more immediately, Q2 FY2026 earnings (June 2026) showing Firefly-attributed ARPU uplift crossing the $5/user/month threshold management has guided toward — this is the quantifiable GenAI monetization proof point the market is waiting for Watch Canva's enterprise push combined with an AI-native new entrant creates a credible two-front threat to Adobe's SMB/mid-market segment — if monthly active user growth in Creative Cloud decelerates to below 5% in Q2 2026 earnings, it signals the commoditization narrative has merit and warrants position re-evaluation

#10 ODD Small Cap Oddity Tech Ltd. · Consumer Cyclical MEDIUM WITHIN MONTHS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

Oddity Tech operates a data-driven, direct-to-consumer beauty platform comprising Il Makiage (color cosmetics) and SpoiledChild (skincare and hair). Revenue is generated entirely through e-commerce subscriptions and repurchase programs, using AI-powered skin analysis and product recommendation algorithms to match consumers with personalized products. The business model is capital-light with no physical retail footprint and gross margins atypically high for beauty (73%).

Industry: AI-Powered Direct-to-Consumer Beauty  ·  vs: L'Oréal (Maybelline, NYX — traditional retail), e.l.f. Beauty (mass DTC), Fenty Beauty (celebrity DTC)

"Market classifies ODD as 'beauty retail' and misses that it is actually an AI recommendation platform with SaaS-like repurchase economics — 73% gross margins, 80%+ retention, and 25% growth are incompatible with 'retail' sector multiples at PE 7.7"

The market's fundamental error is category classification: ODD is not a beauty retailer (which deserves 8-12x PE) but an AI-powered product-recommendation engine that happens to sell beauty products (which deserves 20-25x PE on these unit economics). The 80%+ customer retention rate and 60%+ LTV:CAC ratio are SaaS-grade metrics that no traditional beauty company achieves — these are achievable only because the AI matching algorithm creates genuine product-market fit at the individual consumer level, driving repurchase without advertising spend. At PE 7.7, PEG 0.8, and $17M ADV (institutional-accessible), this is one of the few genuine category mispricing opportunities in the consumer sector today.

Price $14.68 PEG 0.8 P/E 7.7 ROIC — Catalyst SpoiledChild skincare (launched 2023) crossing $200M ARR milestone expected Q2 2026 — this would demonstrate ODD's brand-building capability beyond Il Makiage and support a sum-of-parts valuation that the market currently applies zero credit for the second brand; also, any institutional coverage initiation (currently minimal given $800M market cap) would be a re-rating catalyst Watch Il Makiage customer cohort aging (original 2019-2021 cohorts showing higher churn in 2025 data) without sufficient replacement from new cohorts — if retention falls below 75% in H1 2026 disclosures, the SaaS-like repurchase thesis weakens and the PE 7.7 becomes appropriate rather than a mispricing
Agents: 🏦 Goldman SC 🎯 10-Bagger Hunter 💯 100-Bagger 🔍 Wall St Blindspot

⚠ Key Risks

  • ROIC < 15% kills: RNR (ROIC=8%) passes on FCF/ROE but fails the primary ROIC gate — monitor closely as its inclusion is FCF/ROE-justified, not ROIC-driven; any cat loss event in H2 2026 that erodes book value resets the thesis instantly
  • DLO LatAm FX risk: 47% revenue growth is impressive but LatAm currency devaluations (Argentina, Nigeria) directly compress USD-reported revenues; a 15-20% EM currency basket move would materially impair reported FCF and re-rate the stock
  • DUOL dilution flag (5%/yr): at the boundary of the 10-Bagger dilution threshold; if share-based compensation continues at this pace as growth decelerates from +39% toward the 5yr avg of +20%, the EPS compounding story breaks — watch Q2 2026 SBC as % of revenue

Minimal Street attention for a $1.1B specialty pharma play in high-demand therapeutic area."

Market conflates Collegium with broader opioid industry stigma; missing company-specific differentiation in abuse-deterrent tech and litigation tailwind.

Tax prep is boring, essential, recurring — but market treats it like a sunset business despite 25% op margins and 30% ROIC. Valuation absurdly cheap for quality of moat.

🏢 About
PTCT is a biopharmaceutical company focused on rare genetic diseases, with a pipeline built on translational science targeting nonsense mutations, mitochondrial dysfunction, and other severe monogenic disorders. Revenue is derived from approved gene-therapy and small-molecule programs (Translarna, Emflaza, Upstaza) and a robust clinical pipeline in phase 2/3. Customers are specialty hospitals, genetic disease centers, and international health systems serving ultra-rare patient populations.

Network effect: rare disease registries (e.g., ClinicalTrial.gov, patient advocacy) create data moats—more patients identified → faster trial enrollment → faster value creation."

Precision genomic therapy hits scale as genetic testing adoption accelerates in primary care; market has not fully priced the TAM expansion from newborn screening and carrier testing.

Revenue derives from commercial-stage therapies and a deep pipeline (50+ programs) in CNS, liver, and metabolic disorders. EG5y +30% shows sustainable pipeline momentum. RNAi cost per dose falling ~35% per doubling of manufacturing volume (Wright's Law in biotech manufacturing); network effect: each approved indication (Leqvio, Amvuttra) de-risks the next, attracting more payers and cardiologists to the platform."

RNAi therapeutics crossing from niche (amyloidosis) to blockbuster category (familial hypercholesterolemia); manufacturing scale and payer adoption will compress margins favorably into normalized profitability.

Finds quality companies that fall below institutional radar — too small for major bank coverage, creating persistent mispricings patient investors can exploit.
DCBO Small Cap Docebo Inc. · Technology HIGH Fast Grower ▼
PEG 0.2 (0.14x sales on 14% growth) + 80% gross margin = institutional-quality unit econom
🏢 About
Docebo is a cloud-based learning management system (LMS) platform serving enterprise customers globally. It generates recurring SaaS revenue through platform subscriptions and professional services, serving Fortune 500 companies and mid-market firms across financial services, healthcare, technology, and manufacturing. The company is completely equity-financed with minimal debt and negative buybacks (reinvesting capital).
Industry: Cloud-based Enterprise Learning Software (EdTech/SaaS) · vs: Cornerstone OnDemand, SAP SuccessFactors, Adobe Learning Manager

"$500M Mcap, ~1 analyst coverage (too small for Street's 50M+ revenue focus). SaaS quality metrics: PEG 0.2 + 80% gross margin + 100% revenue consistency + 14% growth = pricing power with zero secular risk. FCF-generative SaaS at 1/3 the trading multiple of peers due to size friction alone."

Pure SaaS business (recurring, 80% margins, consistent cohorts) with institutional-size tailwinds (upskilling budgets) trading at micro-cap multiples because $500M Mcap = no Street bandwidth.

Price $17 P/E 12.3 PEG 0.2 MktCap $0.5B
ANIP Small Cap ANI Pharmaceuticals Inc. · Healthcare HIGH Fast Grower ▼
PEG 0.5 at 44% growth + 69% gross margin + $13M ADV (liquid enough for positioning) + zero
🏢 About
ANI is a specialty pharmaceutical company focused on acquiring and developing a portfolio of off-patent, essential medicines in therapeutic areas including GI, injectable, and ophthalmology. It generates profits through manufacturing and distributing these drugs with limited generic competition and strong pricing power. The company has minimal debt (DE 0.6), strong cash generation, and 100% revenue consistency.
Industry: Specialty Pharmaceuticals (Off-Patent Essential Medicines) · vs: Endo Pharmaceuticals, Assertio Therapeutics, Mayne Pharma

"$1.8B Mcap at the cliff of major Street attention (but still <$2B). Pharma analyst coverage focuses on pipeline risk; ANI's moat (hard-to-replicate manufacturing + regulatory barriers) and 44% growth (accelerating from 26%) escapes Growth-focused screens. PEG 0.5 + 69% gross margin + 100% cohort retention = profitable pharma-scale business priced like smaller biotech."

Specialty pharma with essential-drug moat (inelastic demand, high switching costs, manufacturing moats) and 44% topline growth flagged as 'non-innovative' by Street because it's acquisitions, not R&D.

ODD Small Cap Oddity Tech Ltd. · Technology HIGH Fast Grower ▼
PE 7.7 + PEG 0.8 at 25% growth + 73% gross margin + 100% revenue consistency + $17M ADV =
🏢 About
Oddity Tech is an Israeli-founded direct-to-consumer beauty technology company that uses AI and data analytics to develop and sell personalized skincare and beauty products. It operates a vertically-integrated model combining R&D, manufacturing, and direct distribution via proprietary app and website. Primary customers are Gen-Z/millennial consumers seeking personalized, tech-enabled beauty solutions.
Industry: DTC AI-Powered Beauty Technology · vs: Revlon, Estée Lauder (heritage), Nykaa (DTC competitor)

"$800M Mcap, ~0 Street analysts (Israeli listing on NASDAQ, non-traditional beauty model = institutional drift). High-quality growth: 25% YoY, 27% prior year, 73% gross margin, 100% revenue consistency, PE 7.7 with strong cash position (CR 5.2). AI-driven personalization + app-lock customer data = pricing power and cohort retention that mirrors SaaS without SaaS labels."

SaaS-like unit economics (personalization engine + recurring repurchase) wrapped in beauty retail, so missed by both beauty (non-traditional) and SaaS analysts.

OSPN Small Cap OneSpan Inc. · Technology MEDIUM Slow Grower ▼
PE 5.9 + PEG 0.2 + 21% operating margin + 4.3% dividend yield + $3M ADV on micro-cap = int
🏢 About
OneSpan provides digital identity verification, mobile security, and transaction signing software to financial institutions and enterprises. It generates recurring SaaS revenue through subscription-based cloud solutions and licenses for on-premise deployment. Customers include major banks, fintechs, and payment processors requiring compliance-grade authentication and e-signature.
Industry: Cybersecurity / Identity & Access Management (FinTech Security) · vs: Okta, Ping Identity, Twilio (auth)

"$400M Mcap, ~0–1 Street analysts (enterprise software too small to be a dedicated CRM/analytics play; crypto/fintech sensitivity suppresses coverage post-2022). Deep value: PE 5.9, PEG 0.2, 71% gross margin, 21% operating margin, 4.3% dividend yield = mature, cash-generative software with zero growth but fortress balance sheet. Structural short covering inflated M&A odds (consolidation play) + misclassified as 'slow-growth value' vs. durable revenue stream."

Financially stable enterprise software (100% of revenue recurring, zero debt, 21% OPM) trading at 5.9x PE because stalled growth = Street abandonment, creating buyout/income arbitrage.

Price $11 P/E 5.9 PEG 0.2 MktCap $0.4B
TPB Small Cap Turning Point Brands Inc. · Consumer Defensive HIGH Fast Grower ▼
PEG 0.6 at 28% growth + 21% operating margin + 5.6x cash ratio (zero debt, fortress balanc
🏢 About
Turning Point Brands manufactures and sells alternative tobacco products including cigars, pipe tobacco, and heated tobacco devices. It distributes through independent retailers, gas stations, and convenience stores. The company owns multiple brands and generates strong margins through pricing power and product innovation in the less-regulated alternative segment.
Industry: Alternative Tobacco (Sin Stock / Defensive Cyclical) · vs: Swisher International, Altria (small part), British American Tobacco

"$1.6B Mcap, ~0–1 Street analysts (sin-stock embargo: Morningstar, large index funds, ESG mandates structurally exclude tobacco, depressing coverage and institutional demand). Pristine financials: PE 26.1 / PEG 0.6 at 28% growth, 57% gross margin, 21% OPM, 5.6x cash ratio, zero debt. Regulatory moat (FDA barriers to entry) + price-inelastic demand = earnings power of luxury goods at discount-bin valuation."

High-margin, pricing-power business with regulatory moat (FDA licensing) priced like a sin-stock discount when cash flows are closer to consumer staple quality.

Price $84 P/E 26.1 PEG 0.6 MktCap $1.6B
🌍 Macro Dashboard — FRED data as of 2026-04-17

AI MARKET SYNOPSIS

The market is systematically mispricing two distinct categories: (1) high-ROIC compounders that look 'expensive' on trailing multiples but are cheap on forward FCF and PEG — Adobe, Qualys, Exelixis — because consensus anchors to backward-looking earnings troughs rather than normalized FCF power; (2) small-to-mid cap quality growers with consensus coverage gaps — DLO, ANIP, ODD, DCBO — where institutional size constraints create structural under-ownership. The real opportunity is in businesses with ROIC >25%, PEG <1.0, and identifiable 2026 catalysts that can re-rate multiples, not in generic 'cheap' cyclicals which remain value traps in a 4.32% rate environment.

🌍 GEOPOLITICAL & MACRO CONTEXT

The 10Y Treasury at 4.32% sets a hard hurdle: any equity must justify itself against a 4.32% risk-free rate, meaning FCF yields below 4% require explicit growth premium justification. The yield curve is mildly normal (+55bps), VIX at 17.5 signals complacency rather than fear — not a screaming buy signal for cyclicals, but not a crash-risk environment either. CPI at 3.29% above target with Fed Funds at 3.64% means the Fed is mildly accommodative in real terms but unlikely to cut aggressively; this favors quality FCF generators over rate-sensitive growth stories and makes the 'duration compression' trade in high-multiple tech dangerous without FCF backing.

🔄 Cyclicals

Filter: Cyclical sectors (Industrials, Energy, Materials, Consumer Cyclical) at earnings trough. Lynch: P/E is INVERTED for cyclicals — HIGH or missing P/E = depressed earnings = BUY. LOW P/E = peak earnings = SELL. Best entry: decline decelerating + FCF positive + net debt < 3× EBITDA.
